steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says ephemeral objects are "useful as markers of cultural change" because they can ______ shifts in norms, values, and concerns that traditional objects "may not." The blank needs a verb meaning to reveal or show — something markers do that traditional sources cannot.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — register: means to show or record — exactly what a marker does. Ephemeral objects register shifts that traditional sources may not detect. ✓
  • Choice B — vindicate: means to clear from blame or justify. Nothing in the passage is being defended or exonerated. ✗
  • Choice C — preclude: means to prevent. Saying ephemeral objects prevent shifts makes no sense in context. ✗
  • Choice D — induce: means to cause or bring about. The objects are revealing existing shifts, not causing them. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
